title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: Find the value of: 13‚ąí8‚ąí13+8+69‚ąí8\frac{1}{3-\sqrt{8}} - \frac{1}{3+\sqrt{8}} + \frac{6}{9-8}3‚ąí8‚Äč1‚Äč‚ąí3+8‚Äč1‚Äč+9‚ąí86‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
Two cars start from places A and B, 100 km apart, towards each other and meet in 1 hour. If they travel in the same direction, they meet in 5 hours. Find the speed of the faster car.
questions.explanation: Given: Distance = 100 km. Opposite direction meeting time = 1 hr. Same direction meeting time = 5 hrs. Formula Used: Relative Speed (Opposite) = u + v = D/t Relative Speed (Same) = u - v = D/t Solution: Let speeds be u and v (u > v). u + v =1001 \frac{100}{1}1100‚Äč‚Äč = 100 u - v = 1005\frac{100}{5}5100‚Äč‚Äč = 20 Adding both equations: 2u = 120 ‚üĻ \implies‚üĻ‚Äč u = 60 km/h The faster speed is 60 km/h. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: The difference between CI and SI on a certain sum for 2 years at 15% per annum is ‚āĻ225. Find the sum.
questions.explanation: Given: Difference (D) = ‚āĻ225. Time (t) = 2 years. Rate (R) = 15%. Formula Used: For 2 years, D = P(R100)2P \left(\frac{R}{100}\right)^2P(100R‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Substitute values: ‚Äč225=P(15100)2 225=P(320)2 225=P(9400) P=225√ó4009 P=25√ó400 P=10,000225 = P \left(\frac{15}{100}\right)^2 \\ \ \\225 = P \left(\frac{3}{20}\right)^2\\ \ \\225 = P \left(\frac{9}{400}\right)\\ \ \\P = \frac{225 \times 400}{9}\\ \ \\P = 25 \times 400\\ \ \\P = 10,000225=P(10015‚Äč)2 225=P(203‚Äč)2 225=P(4009‚Äč) P=9225√ó400‚Äč P=25√ó400 P=10,000‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A cricketer has an average of 45 runs in 14 innings. How many runs must he score in the 15th inning to increase his average to 47?
questions.explanation: Given: Average of 14 innings = 45. Target Average after 15 innings = 47. Formula Used: Total = Average \times Number of Innings Solution: Total runs in 14 innings = 14 √ó 45 = 630. Total runs required in 15 innings = 15 √ó 47 = 705. Runs to score in 15th inning = 705 - 630 = 75 Alternate Method: Increase in average = 2. Total increase = 15 √ó 2 = 30. Score = 45 + 30 = 75. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The profit made by selling an article for ‚āĻ1200 is equal to the loss incurred by selling it for ‚āĻ900. What should be the selling price to gain 20%?
questions.explanation: Given: Profit at SP ‚āĻ1200 = Loss at SP ‚āĻ900. Desired Gain = 20%. Formula Used: Profit = SP - CP; Loss = CP - SP Solution: Let CP be x. 1200 - x = x - 900 2x = 2100 x = 1050 CP = ‚āĻ1050. For 20% gain, SP = 1050√ó1201001050 \times \frac{120}{100}1050√ó100120‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 1050√ó1.21050 \times 1.21050√ó1.2‚Äč‚Äč = 1260 Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A merchant mixes 30 kg of tea at ‚āĻ400/kg with 20 kg of tea at ‚āĻ500/kg. He sells the mixture at ‚āĻ480/kg. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Given: Tea A: 30 kg at ‚āĻ400/kg. Tea B: 20 kg at ‚āĻ500/kg. Selling Price of Mixture = ‚āĻ480/kg. Formula Used: Profit % = SP‚ąíCPCP√ó100\frac{SP - CP}{CP} \times 100CPSP‚ąíCP‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Total Cost Price = (30√ó400)+(20√ó500) (30 \times 400) + (20 \times 500) (30√ó400)+(20√ó500)‚Äč= 12000 + 10000 = ‚āĻ22000. Total Weight = 30 + 20 = 50 kg. Average CP per kg = 2200050\frac{22000}{50}5022000‚Äč‚Äč = ‚āĻ440. Profit per kg = 480 - 440 = ‚āĻ40. Profit % = 40440√ó100\frac{40}{440} \times 10044040‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč = 111√ó100 \frac{1}{11} \times 100111‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč = 9.09% Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: What percent of 0.1 cubic meters is 50,000 cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Given: Total volume = 0.1 m3.m^3.m3.‚Äč‚Äč Part volume = 50,000 cm3.cm^3.cm3.‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: 1 m3 m^3m3‚Äč = 1,000,000 cm3cm^3cm3‚Äč‚Äč Percentage = PartTotal√ó100 \frac{\text{Part}}{\text{Total}} \times 100TotalPart‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Convert 0.1 m3m^3m3‚Äč to cm3cm^3cm3‚Äč: ‚Äč0.1√ó1,000,000=100,000 cm3.0.1 \times 1,000,000 = 100,000 \text{ cm}^3.0.1√ó1,000,000=100,000 cm3.‚Äč‚Äč Calculate percentage: ‚Äč50,000100,000√ó100 =12√ó100 =50%\frac{50,000}{100,000} \times 100 \\ \ \\= \frac{1}{2} \times 100 \\ \ \\= 50\%100,00050,000‚Äč√ó100 =21‚Äč√ó100 =50%‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Two chords AB and CD of a circle intersect intersect at a point P inside the circle. If AP = 10 cm, PB = 6 cm, and CP = 4 cm, find the length of PD.
questions.explanation: Given: Chords AB and CD intersect at P. AP = 10 cm, PB = 6 cm, CP = 4 cm. Formula Used: Intersecting Chords Theorem: AP‚čÖPB=CP‚čÖPDAP \cdot PB = CP \cdot PDAP‚čÖPB=CP‚čÖPD‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/4e0c70a6-152a-4a4c-a97b-a15a9fa5aaef.png) ‚ÄčSubstitute the given values into the formula: ‚Äč10‚čÖ6=4‚čÖPD 60=4‚čÖPD PD=604 PD=15 cm10 \cdot 6 = 4 \cdot PD \\ \ \\60 = 4 \cdot PD \\ \ \\PD = \frac{60}{4} \\ \ \\PD = 15 \text{ cm}10‚čÖ6=4‚čÖPD 60=4‚čÖPD PD=460‚Äč PD=15 cm‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: In a factory, Machine A produces 70% of the items and Machine B produces 30%. 2% of A's production is defective and 5% of B's production is defective. If an item is selected at random and found to be defective, what is the probability it came from Machine B?
questions.explanation: Given: P(A) = 0.70, P(B) = 0.30. P(D|A) = 0.02, P(D|B) = 0.05. Formula Used: Bayes' Theorem: P(B|D) = P(D‚ą£B)P(B)P(D‚ą£A)P(A)+P(D‚ą£B)P(B)\frac{P(D|B)P(B)}{P(D|A)P(A) + P(D|B)P(B)}P(D‚ą£A)P(A)+P(D‚ą£B)P(B)P(D‚ą£B)P(B)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Numerator: P(D|B)P(B) = 0.05 √ó 0.30 = 0.015. Denominator: (0.02√ó0.70)+(0.05√ó0.30) (0.02 \times 0.70) + (0.05 \times 0.30)(0.02√ó0.70)+(0.05√ó0.30)‚Äč = 0.014 + 0.015 = 0.029. Probability = 0.0150.029=1529.\frac{0.015}{0.029} = \frac{15}{29}.0.0290.015‚Äč=2915‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: An equilateral triangle and a square have the same perimeter. What is the ratio of the area of the triangle to the area of the square?
questions.explanation: Given: Perimeter of Equilateral Triangle = Perimeter of Square. Formula Used: Area of Triangle = 34a2\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}a^243‚Äč‚Äča2‚Äč‚Äč Area of Square = s2s^2s2‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let side of triangle be a and square be s. ‚Äč3a=4s ‚üĻ a=4s3.3a = 4s \implies a = \frac{4s}{3}.3a=4s‚üĻa=34s‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč Area of Triangle = 34(4s3)2\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \left(\frac{4s}{3}\right)^243‚Äč‚Äč(34s‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč=34‚čÖ16s29= \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \cdot \frac{16s^2}{9}=43‚Äč‚Äč‚čÖ916s2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč=439s2.= \frac{4\sqrt{3}}{9}s^2.=943‚Äč‚Äčs2.‚Äč‚Äč Area of Square = s2. s^2.s2.‚Äč‚Äč Ratio = 43s29:s2=43:9.\frac{4\sqrt{3}s^2}{9} : s^2 = 4\sqrt{3} : 9.943‚Äčs2‚Äč:s2=43‚Äč:9.‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: An arc of a circle of radius 21 cm subtends an angle of 60‚ąė60^\circ60‚ąė‚Äč at the center. Find the length of the arc. (Use ŌÄ=22/7\pi = 22/7ŌÄ=22/7‚Äč)
questions.explanation: Given: Radius r = 21 cm. Angle őł=60‚ąė.\theta = 60^{\circ}.őł=60‚ąė.‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: Length of Arc =őł360√ó2ŌÄr \frac{\theta}{360} \times 2\pi r360őł‚Äč√ó2ŌÄr‚Äč‚Äč Solution: L=60360√ó2√ó227√ó21 L=16√ó44√ó3 L=1326 L=22 cmL = \frac{60}{360} \times 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \\ \ \\L = \frac{1}{6} \times 44 \times 3 \\ \ \\L = \frac{132}{6} \\ \ \\L = 22 \text{ cm}L=36060‚Äč√ó2√ó722‚Äč√ó21 L=61‚Äč√ó44√ó3 L=6132‚Äč L=22 cm‚Äč. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A sphere is inscribed in a hemisphere of radius R such that it touches the curved surface and the plane base. What is the radius of this inscribed sphere?
questions.explanation: Given: Hemisphere radius R. Sphere inscribed touching curved surface and base. Formula Used: Geometric property of inscribed sphere in hemisphere. Solution: Let r be the radius of the inscribed sphere. The sphere sits on the base, so its center is at height r. The top of the sphere touches the highest point of the hemisphere. Total height from base to top of sphere = 2r. This height corresponds to the radius of the hemisphere R (at the pole). Therefore, 2r = R‚ěĒ r = R/2. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A right prism has a regular hexagonal base of side 6 cm. If the height of the prism is 10 cm, find its lateral surface area.
questions.explanation: Given: Base: Regular hexagon, side a = 6 cm. Height h = 10 cm. Formula Used: LSA of Prism = Perimeter of Base √ó Height Solution: Perimeter of hexagon = 6a = 6 √ó 6 = 36 cm. Lateral Surface Area = 36 √ó 10 = 360 cm2. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If sec‚Ā°őł+tan‚Ā°őł=3\sec \theta + \tan \theta = 3secőł+tanőł=3, find the value of tan‚Ā°őł.\tan \theta.tanőł.‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčsec‚Ā°őł+tan‚Ā°őł=3.\sec \theta + \tan \theta = 3.secőł+tanőł=3.‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: sec‚Ā°2őł‚ąítan‚Ā°2őł=1 ‚üĻ (sec‚Ā°őł‚ąítan‚Ā°őł)(sec‚Ā°őł+tan‚Ā°őł)=1\sec^2 \theta - \tan^2 \theta = 1 \\ \ \\\implies (\sec \theta - \tan \theta)(\sec \theta + \tan \theta) = 1sec2őł‚ąítan2őł=1 ‚üĻ(secőł‚ąítanőł)(secőł+tanőł)=1‚Äč Solution: We get sec‚Ā°őł‚ąítan‚Ā°őł=13.\sec \theta - \tan \theta = \frac{1}{3}.secőł‚ąítanőł=31‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč Subtract this from the given equation: ‚Äč(sec‚Ā°őł+tan‚Ā°őł)‚ąí(sec‚Ā°őł‚ąítan‚Ā°őł)=3‚ąí13(\sec \theta + \tan \theta) - (\sec \theta - \tan \theta) = 3 - \frac{1}{3}(secőł+tanőł)‚ąí(secőł‚ąítanőł)=3‚ąí31‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč2tan‚Ā°őł=832\tan \theta = \frac{8}{3}2tanőł=38‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčtan‚Ā°őł=43\tan \theta = \frac{4}{3}tanőł=34‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: From the top of a 60 m high tower, the angle of depression of a car moving away changes from 45^\circ to 30^\circ in 5 seconds. What is the speed of the car?
questions.explanation: Given: Height h = 60 m. Angle changes from 45‚ąė 45^{\circ}45‚ąė‚Äč to 30‚ąė30^{\circ}30‚ąė‚Äč in 5 seconds. Formula Used: Distance = Height √ócot‚Ā°(őł)\times \cot(\theta)√ócot(őł)‚Äč‚Äč Speed = Distance / Time Solution: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/c951bb95-8ad0-40dc-8573-f7a94f090e90.png) Initial distance d1=60cot‚Ā°45‚ąėd_1 = 60 \cot 45^{\circ}d1‚Äč=60cot45‚ąė‚Äč = 60(1) = 60 m. Final distance d2=60cot‚Ā°30‚ąė=603d_2 = 60 \cot 30^{\circ} = 60\sqrt{3} d2‚Äč=60cot30‚ąė=603‚Äč‚Äčm. Distance traveled = 603‚ąí60=60(3‚ąí1)60\sqrt{3} - 60 = 60(\sqrt{3}-1) 603‚Äč‚ąí60=60(3‚Äč‚ąí1)‚Äčm. Speed = 60(3‚ąí1)5=12(3‚ąí1) m/s.\frac{60(\sqrt{3}-1)}{5} = 12(\sqrt{3}-1) \text{ m/s}.560(3‚Äč‚ąí1)‚Äč=12(3‚Äč‚ąí1) m/s.‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: What is the largest 4-digit number that is exactly divisible by 72?
questions.explanation: Given: Divisor = 72. We need the largest 4-digit number divisible by 72. Formula Used: Dividend = Divisor √ó Quotient + Remainder Solution: The largest 4-digit number is 9999. Divide 9999 by 72: 9999 √∑ 72 = 138 with a remainder of 63 To find the largest divisible number, subtract the remainder from 9999: Number = 9999 - 63 Number = 9936 Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Simplify (3+7)2.(\sqrt{3} + \sqrt{7})^2.(3‚Äč+7‚Äč)2.‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: Expression: (3+7)2 (\sqrt{3} + \sqrt{7})^2(3‚Äč+7‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: ‚Äč(a+b)2=a2+b2+2ab(a + b)^2 = a^2 + b^2 + 2ab(a+b)2=a2+b2+2ab‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let a = 3\sqrt{3}3‚Äč‚Äč and b = 7. \sqrt{7}.7‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč Expand the expression: ‚Äč(3)2+(7)2+2(3)(7)(\sqrt{3})^2 + (\sqrt{7})^2 + 2(\sqrt{3})(\sqrt{7})(3‚Äč)2+(7‚Äč)2+2(3‚Äč)(7‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč = 3+7+221 3 + 7 + 2\sqrt{21}3+7+221‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 10+22110 + 2\sqrt{21}10+221‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A can do a work in 12 days and B in 15 days. They contract to do the work for ‚āĻ9,600. With the help of C, they finish it in 5 days. How much does C get?
questions.explanation: Given: A's time = 12 days, B's time = 15 days. A + B + C time = 5 days. Total wages = ‚āĻ9600. Formula Used: Wages are distributed in proportion to efficiency (work done per day). Solution: Let Total Work = 60 units (LCM of 12, 15, 5). Efficiency of A = 60/12 = 5 units/day. Efficiency of B = 60/15 = 4 units/day. Efficiency of (A+B+C) = 60/5 = 12 units/day. Efficiency of C = 12 - (5 + 4) = 3 units/day. Ratio of shares A:B:C = 5:4:3. Total parts = 12. C's share = 312√ó9600\frac{3}{12} \times 9600123‚Äč√ó9600‚Äč‚Äč = 14√ó9600\frac{1}{4} \times 960041‚Äč√ó9600‚Äč‚Äč = ‚āĻ2400 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A and B invest ‚āĻ50,000 and ‚āĻ70,000. After one year, the total profit is ‚āĻ24,000. What is A's share of the profit?
questions.explanation: Given: Investment of A = ‚āĻ50,000. Investment of B = ‚āĻ70,000. Total Profit = ‚āĻ24,000. Formula Used: Profit Share ‚ąĚ\propto‚ąĚ‚Äč Investment √óTime Solution: Since the time period is the same (1 year), the ratio of profit is the ratio of investments. Ratio A : B = 50,000 : 70,000 = 5 : 7. Total parts = 5 + 7 = 12. A's share = 512√ó24,000\frac{5}{12} \times 24,000125‚Äč√ó24,000‚Äč‚Äč = 5√ó2,000 5 \times 2,0005√ó2,000‚Äč‚Äč = ‚āĻ10,000 Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: What is the x-intercept of the line 5x + 3y = 15?
questions.explanation: Given: Line: 5x + 3y = 15. Formula Used: For x-intercept, set y = 0. Solution: 5x + 3(0) = 15 5x = 15 x = 3. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: PA and PB are tangents to a circle from an external point P. If the length of PA is 12 cm, what is the length of PB?
questions.explanation: Given: Tangents PA and PB from P. PA = 12 cm. Formula Used: Lengths of tangents from an external point to a circle are equal. Solution: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/6442bb86-07d8-4ea2-872b-d656bd744de2.png) PA = PB. Since PA = 12 cm, PB = 12 cm. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: In triangle ABC, the area is 60 cm2 and the product of the lengths of its three sides is 2400. Find the circumradius of the triangle.
questions.explanation: Given: Area A = 60 cm2. Product of sides abc = 2400. Formula Used: Circumradius R = abc4A\frac{abc}{4A}4Aabc‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ‚ÄčR=24004√ó60 R=2400240 R=10 cm.R = \frac{2400}{4 \times 60} \\ \ \\R = \frac{2400}{240} \\ \ \\R = 10 \text{ cm}.R=4√ó602400‚Äč R=2402400‚Äč R=10 cm.‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The mean of a set of 5 numbers is 20. If a number 'p' is added, the mean becomes 22. If another number 'q' replaces 'p', the mean of the original 5 plus 'q' becomes 24. Find p + q.
questions.explanation: Given: Mean of 5 = 20. p added, Mean = 22. q replaces p, Mean = 24. Formula Used: Sum = Mean √ó N Solution: Sum(5) = 100. Sum(5+p) = 22 √ó 6 = 132 ‚ěĒ p = 32. Sum(5+q) = 24√ó6=144 ‚üĻ q=44.24 \times 6 = 144 \implies q = 44.24√ó6=144‚üĻq=44.‚Äč‚Äč p + q = 32 + 44 = 76. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A vendor buys 200 toys at ‚āĻ150 each. He finds 10% of them broken and sells them at a 40% loss. The rest are sold at a 20% profit. What is his overall profit or loss percentage?
questions.explanation: Given: Total toys = 200. Broken = 10% = 20 toys. Sold at -40% profit. Good = 90% = 180 toys. Sold at +20% profit. Formula Used: Weighted Average Profit % = (n1√óp1)+(n2√óp2)n1+n2 \frac{(n_1 \times p_1) + (n_2 \times p_2)}{n_1 + n_2}n1‚Äč+n2‚Äč(n1‚Äč√óp1‚Äč)+(n2‚Äč√óp2‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Since CP is same for all: Overall % = (20√ó‚ąí40)+(180√ó20)200 \frac{(20 \times -40) + (180 \times 20)}{200}200(20√ó‚ąí40)+(180√ó20)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = ‚ąí800+3600200\frac{-800 + 3600}{200}200‚ąí800+3600‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 2800200\frac{2800}{200}2002800‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 14%. Overall profit is 14%. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Given 7+x+7‚ąíx7+x‚ąí7‚ąíx\frac{\sqrt{7+x} + \sqrt{7-x}}{\sqrt{7+x} - \sqrt{7-x}}7+x‚Äč‚ąí7‚ąíx‚Äč7+x‚Äč+7‚ąíx‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 2, then what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äč7+x+7‚ąíx7+x‚ąí7‚ąíx=2\frac{\sqrt{7+x} + \sqrt{7-x}}{\sqrt{7+x} - \sqrt{7-x}} = 2\\7+x‚Äč‚ąí7‚ąíx‚Äč7+x‚Äč+7‚ąíx‚Äč‚Äč=2‚Äč‚Äč Concept Used: Componendo and Dividendo rule. Formula Used: If ab=cd \frac{a}{b} = \frac{c}{d}\\ba‚Äč=dc‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč then a+ba‚ąíb=c+dc‚ąíd\frac{a+b}{a-b} = \frac{c+d}{c-d}\\a‚ąíba+b‚Äč=c‚ąídc+d‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Apply Componendo and Dividendo. Let the RHS be 21\frac{2}{1}12‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč(7+x+7‚ąíx)+(7+x‚ąí7‚ąíx)(7+x+7‚ąíx)‚ąí(7+x‚ąí7‚ąíx)=2+12‚ąí1 27+x27‚ąíx=3 7+x7‚ąíx=3\frac{(\sqrt{7+x} + \sqrt{7-x}) + (\sqrt{7+x} - \sqrt{7-x})}{(\sqrt{7+x} + \sqrt{7-x}) - (\sqrt{7+x} - \sqrt{7-x})} = \frac{2+1}{2-1}\\ \ \\\frac{2\sqrt{7+x}}{2\sqrt{7-x}} = 3\\ \ \\\frac{\sqrt{7+x}}{\sqrt{7-x}} = 3\\ (7+x‚Äč+7‚ąíx‚Äč)‚ąí(7+x‚Äč‚ąí7‚ąíx‚Äč)(7+x‚Äč+7‚ąíx‚Äč)+(7+x‚Äč‚ąí7‚ąíx‚Äč)‚Äč=2‚ąí12+1‚Äč 27‚ąíx‚Äč27+x‚Äč‚Äč=3 7‚ąíx‚Äč7+x‚Äč‚Äč=3‚Äč‚Äč Square both sides. 7+x7‚ąíx\frac{7+x}{7-x}7‚ąíx7+x‚Äč‚Äč = 9 7 + x = 9(7 - x) 7 + x = 63 - 9x 10x = 56 x = 5.6 So the correct answer is (a)
